title: [WIP] Begin wiring in USERNS Support into podman

comments.body: Will exec need any work, or does runc handle the userns part of it?
No idea.  Probably.  going through and fixing issus as I realize them
we need to ensure the directories created by podman are owned by root in the userNS.  In addition to https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/171, it should be enough to get userNS working
That's a good point. All the bind mounts libpod adds (secrets, /etc/resolv.conf, etc) and the SHM mount will need permissions adjusted.
The patch currently fixes everything except secrets, and I am working on that.

> The patch currently fixes everything except secrets, and I am working on that.

I had the same issue as https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/171, the `Chown` is not enough. it also needs that the parent directories are accessible
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 6f51a5b) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

I added some of @giuseppe patches to loosen the permissions on the parent directories and now userns partially works.
```
./bin/podman run --security-opt label=disable --net none --rm --uidmap 0:20000:60000 --gidmap 0:20000:60000 alpine cat /proc/self/uid_map
         0      20000      60000
```

We have discuss the permission changes on the parent directories, to see if we can do something better then this.
Also not sure why this is not working with SELinux.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 9aafc25) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably fa8442e) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@rhatdan Can we get a way to specify mappings via file, instead of via multiple flags? Might be easier than adding a bunch of flags to every `podman run` call

@mheon Not sure that a file would make this easier.  With the current implementation you should be able to do a single --uidmap or --subuidname and it will run in a usernamespace with matching uid/gid content.  I guess putting a complicated mapping into a file would help, but that would also complicate the documentation.

I talked to @nalind Yesterday about a potential solution where the tools 

bind mount /var/lib/container/storage/overlay* on to /run/containers/storage/ID/*
Which would be setup world searchable and then these paths would be passed to runc.  If this works we would not need to lower the protection on the system directories.  

@giuseppe could you take a stab at trying something like that out.  Not sure if @nalind got a chance.  I am in meetings and on PTO next two days (Son's Wedding).

enjoy that!

Yes, sure I can try this solution and I think it will work, but we will introduce a completely different handling for non root users and we will need to worry to clean these mounts as well.

Do we know what are the risks for making `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay2` accessible?  If `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay2/ID` is still owned by the root in the container and has mode 700, no other users can get access to it.

@giuseppe There is other content under /var/lib/containers/storage that could lead to information leak, additional world writeable file etc.  I agree you have secured the overlay directory (We don't have overaly2 dir in containers/storage.)
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 313e5e8) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

`/run/containers/storage` has another level `$DRIVER-containers`, should I use that?

/cc @nalind

That's the parent of the `ContainerRunDirectory()`, which I think gets used as the BundlePath in CRI-O.  A new directory next to `$driver-containers`, and relaxing the permissions of `/var/run/containers` and its `storage` subdirectory, might work.
PR opened here: https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/173
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 27107fd) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

@rhatdan could you cherry-pick `"networking, userNS: configure the network namespace after create"` from https://github.com/giuseppe/libpod/tree/userns ?

It solves the issue with the network namespace that is created by runc but configured later, so it was created in the correct userNS:

```
# ./bin/podman run --security-opt="label=disable"  --rm --uidmap 0:1:60000 --gidmap 0:20000:60000 alpine ping -c 1 redhat.com
PING redhat.com (10.4.204.55): 56 data bytes
64 bytes from 10.4.204.55: seq=0 ttl=246 time=168.030 ms

--- redhat.com ping statistics ---
1 packets transmitted, 1 packets received, 0% packet loss
round-trip min/avg/max = 168.030/168.030/168.030 ms
```

The `--security-opt="label=disable"` is not needed with the last runc.
@giuseppe Pulled in your patch and updated to latest code.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #647) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.

I've added a patch to my branch that implements an intermediate mount namespace so we won't need any change in containers/storage.  I've also changed your patch to not bring in any change to vendor.  If you could please reset to my branch and we can work from there
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 6ac8a24) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 39a7a77) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
